rationale: Quantified axis with specific revenue impact ($4M) and time compression (3 months to 1 month). Passes CLAIM-DEFINITION decontextualization: names actor (Hormozi), company context (two senior reps), and carries mechanism.
Conditional mechanism claim about goal achievement via physics-first reasoning. Well-decontextualized with conditions (internet, inventory, logistics, power). Type=conditional is appropriate.
Mechanism: low agreeableness enables outlier returns because consensus-seeking is incompatible with deviation. Has explicit because-clause about psychological trait and outcome.
Mechanism: people repeat words due to social reinforcement, not semantic content. Clear causal chain (reinforcer → behavior).
Mechanism: viewing language as operant behavior is the lever for changing communication patterns. Has explicit because-clause (visibility → change prerequisite).
Assertion about prerequisite (definitions) for problem-solving. Decontextualized and load-bearing but lacks causal mechanism—thus L2 not L2a.
Mechanism: coaching creates pre-qualified pipeline by reducing deal-sourcing friction. Has explicit because-clause.

Assertion with embedded mechanism about psychological ownership. Decontextualized with conditions (founder needs GM autonomy, multi-location service business). Load-bearing on how to set up GM incentives.
Mechanism with quantified axis: $6k/year consulting creates pre-qualified acquisition pipeline. Has explicit mechanism + numbers.

Conditional claim: paid ads theoretically better but practically cold outreach better for beginners. Has mechanism (ads require skill, cold outreach only effort) but primarily a conditional comparison.
Quantified axis: 2-3x per week posting frequency. Has specific conditions (early-stage outbound) and mechanism (social profile acts as credibility proof).
Assertion: social profiles are de facto website for cold outreach prospects. Decontextualized with conditions but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion about referral underpreparedness in professional services. Decontextualized, load-bearing on sales process, has embedded mechanism but primarily assertive.
Quantified axis: $1,500/month recovers ~90 hours/month for average American. Clear anchors on cost and time recovered.
Mechanism: triple compound of guilt/opulence/entitlement blocks spending; peer exposure dismantles it. Has explicit because-clause (compound barrier + peer model solution).
Assertion about experiential spending as untapped territory for high earners. Has mechanism (produces compounding happiness) but primarily an assertion about opportunity.
Assertion: required mindset for strategic spending oriented toward life quality vs. status. No mechanism, no decontextualization beyond description. Borderline L2 but load-bearing on mindset.
Conditional claim: brand-risk objections create ceiling on larger-founder acquisition. Well-decontextualized with explicit conditions and mechanism (reputation already asset worth protecting).
Mechanism: reframe brand risk by linking business growth to personal brand strength. Has explicit because-clause (growth increases brand, so DMs increase brand too).
Quantified: ~50% acceptance rate after reframe sufficient for agency growth. Has axis (acceptance rate) and anchor points.
Conditional: targeting smaller founders eliminates brand-risk objection. Decontextualized with clear conditions and mechanism but primarily tactical segmentation.
Assertion: excellent businesses have clear standards, poor ones lack them; owner responsible. Decontextualized, load-bearing on culture/standards but no mechanism.
Assertion: owners fail to operationalize standards into measurable behaviors. Decontextualized, load-bearing on execution but no mechanism.
Mechanism: owner non-compliance defeats standard enforcement. Has explicit because-clause (teams calibrate to owner behavior, not words; gap signals standard not truly held).
Conditional: leader must demonstrate behavior change before requesting it. Has mechanism (credibility derives from demonstrated compliance) but conditional framing.
Mechanism: multi-location degradation due to founder attention split. Has explicit because-clause (founder presence reduction → margin fall proportional to attention reduction).
Assertion: organic/SEO growth reflects favorable market condition not scalable practice. Decontextualized with conditions, has mechanism but primarily assertive about market conditions.

Assertion: top personal-service providers leave taking clients unless business structures brand loyalty. Decontextualized with conditions, has mechanism (customer trust tied to individual) but primarily assertive.
Conditional: proven model = capital-constrained not model-constrained problem. Decontextualized with specific context (med spa with 3 locations) but lacks explicit mechanism.
Mechanism: commission structure creates financial incentive to preserve costs not reduce them. Has explicit because-clause (broker income scales with premium, reduction reduces revenue).

Assertion: Hormozi asks 'what behavior change' before consuming content. Decontextualized personal practice but not a load-bearing claim about the world—borderline L4' Practice but included as personal framework.
Mechanism: perceived deprivation determined by reference group not absolute wealth. Has explicit because-clause (larger gap from reference group benchmark = greater motivation).
Mechanism: replicate before iterating to prove internalization. Has explicit because-clause (replication proves correct understanding; changes only then attribute to improvement).
Assertion: Hormozi and Leila share all knowledge exposure. Decontextualized, load-bearing on partnership model but no mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: integrity as non-negotiable superseding high-value opportunities. Decontextualized personal value but no mechanism about world/business.
Quantified axis: U.S. household wealth distribution ($32 of $100 in top 1%, $2 in bottom 50%). Multiple anchors per CLAIM-DEFINITION L2b.
Quantified: top 10% of U.S. households have $1M+ net worth (1 in 10). Axis and anchors on wealth prevalence.
Quantified axis: Pareto nested (20% = 80% profit, top 4% = 64%, top 1% = 51%). Multiple anchors on profit distribution.
Assertion with numbers: 5-10x tier jump with ~20% ascent rate. Has mechanism (reflects actual wealth percentile differences) but primarily assertive pricing rule.
Quantified: 1000-customer base example (800 @ $10, 200 @ $100, 40 @ $1k, 8 @ $10k with revenue anchors). Multiple regions and values per L2b definition.
Assertion: Acquisition.com uses $100/100/5k/35k/135k tiers. Decontextualized (named company/tiers) but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: wealthy people shop high-to-low (quality-first). Decontextualized with mechanism (quality/time constraint) but primarily assertive.
Comparison: underpricing ($100 to $1k buyer) loses $900 mostly profit; overpricing ($1k to $100 buyer) loses $100 sale. Has mechanism but comparison-type claim.
Mechanism: high-priced anchor improves conversion on core by making core look like bargain. Has explicit because-clause (contrast effect shifts reference point).
Mechanism: low CAC misleading because LTV (churn, spend, burden) only reveals over weeks. Has explicit because-clause (cost visibility immediate, LTV delayed creates systematic bias).
Comparison: personal trainers convert cheap but bad avatars. Has mechanism (cost visibility vs. hidden burden) but comparison-framed and somewhat anecdotal.
Mechanism: work capacity trainable via adaptation. Has explicit because-clause (higher volume triggers adaptation raising threshold, analogous to fitness).
Conditional: burnout framed as temporary capacity limit (die, injury, or adapt). Has mechanism but conditional framing makes it L2.
Mechanism: scalable sales based on prospect questions not salesman answers. Has explicit because-clause (process independence from expertise makes it trainable/replicable).
Assertion: script should give minimum info needed. Has mechanism (reduces domain knowledge requirement, keeps focus on pain) but assertive framing.
Mechanism: founder closing undermines rep process by making founder hero not prospect. Has explicit because-clause (founder authority positions founder expertise as value driver not pain discovery).
Comparison: rep role vs. founder consultative close (hospital receptionist analogy). Decontextualized comparison but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Comparison: $1,500 increment ineffective vs. $5-10k correct tier. Has mechanism (buyers evaluate categorical jumps not increments) but comparison-type.
Mechanism: mass-market low-price service requires full automation. Has explicit because-clause (labor scales linearly, price capped low, margin insufficient).
Assertion: elevating action to sacred status is cognitive error. Decontextualized, has mechanism (identity threat increases resistance) but primarily assertive.
Conditional: change behavior if cost to actor negligible and benefit to other significant. Has mechanism (asymmetric ratio makes refusal irrational) but conditional framing.
Mechanism: DTC commoditization without brand moat. Has explicit because-clause (identical talent/product/bidding = zero advantage; only brand asymmetry possible).
Comparison: CRO like steroids (2-3x one-time lift, then ceiling). Has mechanism (CRO improves rate at volume; becomes new floor; brand needed for volume gain) but comparison-type.
Assertion: influence = high likelihood compliance with requests. Decontextualized operational definition but not a load-bearing claim—borderline L2.
Mechanism: status = control of scarce reinforcers in environment. Has explicit because-clause (context-dependent because status disappears when resource control disappears).
Assertion: Likeness = perceived psychological/demographic similarity. Decontextualized operational definition, has mechanism (association with prior positive figures) but primarily definitional.
Quantified: gender split in audience (Alex 89% male, Layla 54% female) for same-topic creators. Has axis (gender) and anchors despite both operating in same topic.
Assertion: single event satisfies multiple SPCL components simultaneously. Decontextualized (selling company publicly) but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Mechanism: product quality is restaurant marketing budget. Has explicit because-clause (word-of-mouth drives discovery; poor product fails to generate it; marketing cannot substitute).
Comparison: restaurants and software converge (product only matters long-run). Decontextualized comparison but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Mechanism: restaurant marketing is premium on ingredients/service/systems increasing word-of-mouth. Has explicit because-clause (quality investments → organic sharing replaces paid spend).
Assertion: restaurants hard for LTV-to-CAC measurement due to data/margin challenges. Decontextualized but not load-bearing on business decision—borderline L2.
Comparison: brick-and-mortar word-of-mouth principle more exacerbated in restaurants. Comparison-type with context but lacks explicit mechanism.
Mechanism: churn = hiring rate in 17-rep team masks process problem. Has explicit because-clause (without enforcement, team loses performers continuously; replacement consumes bandwidth).
Conditional: entrenched culture requires 50%+ firing to reset. Decontextualized with conditions, has mechanism (self-reinforcing culture) but conditional framing.
Assertion: inbound sales business = media-buying + sales with sales excellence as single constraint. Decontextualized with conditions but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: feeling and action decoupled; don't need to feel good to win. Decontextualized, load-bearing on psychology, has mechanism but primarily assertive.
Assertion: high-achievers hold grandiosity AND crippling insecurity simultaneously. Decontextualized psychological profile but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: high-achievers possess strong impulse control (third trait). Decontextualized psychological observation but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Mechanism: early small wins build belief in action viability. Has explicit because-clause (wins provide evidence regardless of feelings; sustains behavior loop).
Assertion: people don't know why they do things; stated reasons post-hoc rationalizations. Decontextualized but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: define input-output equation before scaling. Decontextualized, load-bearing but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Mechanism: control skill for job exit must be unlearned to scale. Has explicit because-clause (entry rewarded by control; scaling needs leverage; reward creates barrier).
Assertion: founder delegation sequence (8 steps from delivery to financing). Decontextualized, load-bearing but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Conditional: 100x outcome needs leverage not harder work. Has mechanism (biological ceiling on single person) but conditional framing.
Quantified: Hormozi has 10,000x input baseline via leverage. Has axis (input multiple) and anchors (1x gym phase vs 10,000x current).
Assertion: people cannot identify true root cause of behavior; stated reasons narrative. Decontextualized but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Mechanism: 'bad things come in threes' false; one event triggers cascading harm via behavior. Has explicit because-clause (abandoning self-control after setback creates reinforcing descent).
Assertion: single event contaminating other domains worse than one event. Decontextualized, has mechanism (setbacks like cancer) but assertive framing.
Assertion: low resilience = using bad day as excuse to misbehave. Decontextualized, has mechanism (self-control failure) but observational.
Mechanism: negative cascade pattern (job loss → weight gain → decline). Has explicit because-clause (uncontained setback removes reinforcement source, weakening capacity in remaining domains).
Conditional: shame/deprivation drivers evaluated by functional output not emotional valence. Has mechanism but conditional framing.

Mechanism: obsessing over efficiency prevents volume keeps operator small. Has explicit because-clause (optimization causes under-investment; proportional relationship means small input = small output).
Assertion: scaling creative cost far smaller than incremental revenue. Decontextualized with conditions but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Conditional: resource gap (editors, etc.) solvable with math. Decontextualized, has mechanism (economics almost always say yes) but conditional framing.
Quantified: 100x effort sometimes yields ~1,000,000x payoff. Has axis (effort-payoff ratio) and anchors on multipliers.
Mechanism: being best produces exponential returns via category leadership. Has explicit because-clause (leader captures category definition, premium pricing, brand advantage).
Assertion: most people underestimate work capacity. Decontextualized with conditions, has mechanism (subjective sensation calibrated to prior experience) but assertive.
Comparison: early standards (8 hrs) vs current (hundreds hrs). Comparison-type, has mechanism (rising standards cause retroactive embarrassment) but observational.
Mechanism: quality-bounded definition of done produces different quality than time-bounded. Has explicit because-clause (time-bounded caps quality; quality-bounded exhausts improvement paths).
Quantified: book production (19 drafts, 4 rewrites, 106 images, third-grade level). Has axis (revision iterations) and multiple quantified anchors.
Mechanism: rising standards drive 100x more work (internal not external). Has explicit because-clause (rising internal bar creates gap between output and perception, driving effort).
Mechanism: tiny absolute performance edge → massive economic reward (rank-order tournament). Has explicit because-clause (rank 1 reward qualitatively different from rank 2).
Assertion: successive time increments reveal hidden improvement paths (video editing thought experiment). Decontextualized but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: work shapes you more than results; skills persist through failure. Decontextualized, load-bearing, has mechanism but assertive framing.
Quantified: 5 years gym chain (baseline) → next 12 months ~5x profit. Has axis (profit relative) and anchors on time/multiple.
Mechanism: high-output misunderstood as overwork when work = leisure. Has explicit because-clause (undifferentiated work/enjoyment eliminates motivational friction for high volume).
Assertion: what person becomes only durable output of life. Philosophical assertion, load-bearing on motivation but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: short-term physical strain doesn't cause burnout. Decontextualized with conditions but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: speaker never regretted trying harder. Personal experience report, weak generalization—borderline L2 but included.
Assertion: hard times feel long, epic stories feel like lifetime. Decontextualized, has mechanism (temporal perception distortion) but assertive.
Assertion: no conflict between work-lovers and balance advocates. Decontextualized with conditions, has mechanism (no experienced cost) but assertive.
Comparison: factual confirmation vs motivational reinforcement during peak stress. Comparison-type, has mechanism (evidence-based > emotion-based credibility) but observational.
Assertion: survived everything so far justifies crediting capacity. Decontextualized but lacks mechanism—thus L2.
Mechanism: lower intelligence can compensate via higher daily iteration. Has explicit because-clause (higher volume compresses inter-iteration gaps, multiplying effective learning rate).
Quantified: Gym Launch ad testing (~$50k, 100 ads, top 5 delivered, 10-20 markets). Multiple anchors on testing investment and output.
Assertion: membership value = risk of own testing + direct revenue gain. Decontextualized framework but lacks explicit mechanism—thus L2.
Assertion: unused items decrease perceived value via cognitive overload. Decontextualized with conditions, has mechanism (unused = no value) but assertive.
Mechanism: consumable research/analysis is highest-leverage media. Has explicit because-clause (time-intensive work converted to ready-to-use deliverable; members trade fee for saved time).
Assertion: magical product = invisible complexity + frictionless output. Decontextualized, has mechanism (members value frictionless outcomes not effort visibility) but assertive.
